revisedPrompt:
Create a wide 16:9 production-ready timeout-and-retry illustration for an EX-0007 visual redesign, using the attached references. Use ONLY the attached magenta sprite sheet as the character identity reference: faithfully preserve the exact original identities, clothing, colors, accessories, and recognizable silhouettes of (1) the young ground courier from the top row—orange hair, teal scarf and tunic, orange shorts, green satchel, gloves and boots—and (2) the human flying rival from the bottom row—teal hair, goggles, green scarf, coral shirt, pale blue trousers, gloves, green satchel, and handmade pale-green winged flying craft. Completely ignore and remove the sprite sheet’s magenta background. Use the attached mountain-pass landscape as the environmental continuity reference: bright blue sky, layered blue-green mountain valley, pale rocky cliffs, conifers, shrubs, soft clouds, clean hand-drawn outlines, flat pastel colors, whimsical storybook game aesthetic. Do not reproduce the unrelated urban/rail scene or its characters.

Original composition, side-profile course. Reserve the entire LEFT 45% as calm, low-detail negative space suitable for live HTML error explanation and retry controls: mostly open pale-blue sky and very subtle distant mountain shapes, with no character, focal prop, text, or busy vegetation there. Place all storytelling safely in the right 55%, with generous crop-safe margins.

On the lower-right side, show the young ground courier standing safely on an intact grassy trail just before ONE clearly fallen earth section: a modest, readable break where a short portion of the trail has slumped downward into a shallow rocky dip, with stable rounded edges and a few settled stones. It must feel like a temporary route interruption, not a dangerous chasm. The courier is in side profile facing right, feet planted securely well back from the edge, calm but determined, body slightly ready to move again, hands relaxed/ready, expression encouraging and resilient—not sad, ashamed, frightened, injured, or punished.

Across the broken section on the far-right upper distance, show the human flying rival already reaching the far side, small but clearly recognizable, gliding or just arriving above a safe distant ledge in the same pale-green handmade flying craft. Keep the rival dignified and non-taunting. Use depth through scale and atmospheric perspective. The scene should communicate timeout, pause, and retry through visual storytelling only: the course is temporarily interrupted, while the courier is safe and ready to try again.

Mood: calm, dignified, encouraging, optimistic, gentle morning clarity. Clean hand-drawn linework, flat pastel fills, restrained texture, cohesive whimsical storybook game art. Keep both figures fully visible and crop-safe. No danger, falling person, injury, panic, shame, punishment, taunting, scoreboard, race ranking, baked-in text, letters, numbers, UI, buttons, title, logo, watermark, branding, copied characters, copied composition, magenta, or urban/rail elements.

The courier remains determined and ready to try again; the mood is calm, dignified, and encouraging, never punitive. Leave the left 45% calm and low-detail for live HTML error explanation and retry control. Keep figures crop-safe.
